title: The Vertically Repeated Motel Forever

features.name: the stairwell of tonight
features.desc-on-touch: you climb and every landing shows the same door, the same light beneath it, the same smell of air conditioning and old carpet; you realize you stopped climbing some time ago and are still at the same step

features.name: the identical room sequence
features.desc-on-touch: each door opens to a room you've seen before, or will see again; the furniture is always slightly rearranged; after the eighth identical room you stop checking for differences

features.name: the vertical infinity marker
features.desc-on-touch: a number on the wall—you can't quite read it because it keeps incrementing, or decrementing, or it was always that number and you keep discovering it for the first time

features.name: the archive node in every hallway
features.desc-on-touch: a casing bolted to the wall, identical on every floor; the display shows the room number, the time (always 9:47 PM), and a file named 'checkout_tomorrow.txt', which you know by heart and have never opened

features.name: the comparison wall
features.desc-on-touch: here you notice that this floor's carpet is a subtly different shade of rust; this floor's ice machine is humming a different pitch; the differences are minute but they have compounded into infinite variation within infinite repetition

inscription: every floor is tonight, and tonight arrives again in every floor; the motel has learned that repetition is not absence of change but change at a scale too small to be believed

file.name: Motel_Guest_Logbook_Floors_23-24_Sept_1989.txt
file.note: a register of guests, all checked in for one night, all still here; their names repeat on every floor's entry, and their checkout dates are all marked as 'tomorrow'

reverie.title: REVERIE — the night that refuses to end
narr: you sit on the edge of the bed with her, the same bed
on every floor, the same television playing the same test
pattern. "we could leave," she says. "or we could stop
trying to." you watch the room repeat itself in memory and
in space. "tonight," she whispers, "is infinite. and you're
in it. that's enough. that's more than enough."

exits.label: back down the stairwell to the lobby
where the repetition breaks into variation
toward the penthouse that is also tonight
